1.1: Haggai prophesied in 520 BCE, the second year of Darius I of Persia — 16 years after the first returnees arrived in Jerusalem. The temple reconstruction had stalled: the returning exiles had built their own houses while the temple remained unfinished (1:4). Haggai's four oracles (1:1-11; 2:1-9; 2:10-19; 2:20-23) call the community to prioritize the temple and promise divine blessing as a result. His contemporary was Zechariah, whose visions (chs. 1-8) addressed the same restoration community. Together they provide the theological framework for the post-exilic community's task: rebuilding the worship center through which YHWH's presence among his people would be maintained until the coming of the one greater than the temple.
